name: Ray's Bus Svc Inc
recent_news: No public news, web presence, or contractor listings found; only a small-business filing at 4545 New Holland St, Hudsonville, MI (2026 web search).
fit_notes: Possible micro-operator (~39 employees) in Summit's MI footprint, but no digital footprint or confirmed district contracts; would require offline diligence to verify it is even an active school-bus contractor vs. a dba/holding entity.

name: M & K Bus Svc LLC
districts_or_customers: Bartelso/Breese Central / Aviston-area schools in Clinton County, IL
recent_news: Active local school-bus operator referenced in a 2024 Bartelso/Breese Central transportation contract document alongside Varel Bus; small Facebook page (private/family operator profile).
fit_notes: Sub-scale rural IL family operator (~30 buses) running yellow-bus contracts for small Clinton County districts; potential cost-of-capital tuck-in but very small ticket and likely owner-driver model.

name: Blanca's School Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Fort Bend County / Sugar Land, TX area (charter/contract school transportation)
recent_news: FMCSA SAFER snapshot shows ACTIVE intrastate-only TX carrier, USDOT 1966158, 5 power units, 4 drivers, passenger cargo; no M&A activity.
fit_notes: Very small (5-bus) home-address LLC in Sugar Land; below typical Summit tuck-in threshold and unlikely to be a meaningful district-contract operator — closer to a charter/activity sub-contractor.

name: Totman Enterprises Inc
districts_or_customers: Marshfield Public Schools (MA) — long-standing transportation contractor; also operates a construction/sewer line of business under the same corporate entity.
recent_news: Profiled in School Transportation News during the 2009 recession when Martha Totman forewent worker pay raises to cut $100K from the Marshfield contract; FMCSA snapshot shows 28 power units / 20 drivers and active interstate passenger authority (last MCS-150 04/2024).
fit_notes: Legitimate 60-year-old South Shore MA family school-bus contractor (Marshfield Public Schools anchor) with ~28 buses and an in-house CDL school-bus training program; owner-operated by Martha Totman (multi-decade tenure) so succession is a plausible angle for a Summit tuck-in, but diligence must separate the school-bus operation from the affiliated sewer/construction business.

name: Sijz Transport Corp
districts_or_customers: Morris County / Montville, NJ area; listed in NJ DOE 2020-21 School Bus Contractor Codes; reportedly operates S2-plate vans for special-needs students
recent_news: Active NJ DOE-registered school-bus contractor with secondary Lake Hiawatha (Parsippany-Troy Hills) operating address; not a member of NJSBCA as of 2026.
fit_notes: Small Morris County NJ special-needs/Type-S contractor (~25 employees) in dense NJ school-bus market; could be a low-cost add-on for a Summit NJ build-out, but no public principal info means heavy offline qualification needed.
